title: Very limited database
text: Out of the 20 cookbooks I have and use, only 3 were available in the indexed library. The rest were either not indexed or unrecognized.

title: Cooking with myself.
text: Rekindled passion for my beloved cookbooks. Slow down, chop, breathe…♥️📚

title: decent, but could go further for the price
text: I don't expect full recipes here, but I do wish there was more flexibility in the app. Tagging books by a category we choose would help a lot, especially for filtering. (Such as, I have a few books that are more challenging "project" cooking - so I'd love to be able to tag a book so it doesn't show up when I want a weeknight meal.) I wish there were other view options than one long list of recipe titles - like small thumbnails or something. Obviously it would be great to sort by cook time or difficulty if applicable, but I understand that would be very hard to index. The other big thing, which I don't think would be possible, is to get the book photos instead of user-submitted photos for the recipes. Almost none have photos, and those that do aren't appetizing, making me not want to cook it. I really appreciate what the app does, and the amount it DID index was good (not great for more obscure or vintage books, which I understand.) But it's not functional enough for me to subscribe yet.

title: Terrific App!
text: The CookShelf App has made my cookbook collection so much more useable! Being able to search by ingredients is a game-changer and I also find it very helpful to quickly search for specific recipes from specific books. I love being able to save notes, ratings and changes to recipes I’ve cooked, as well—all in the App. I have rediscovered some real gems on my own bookshelves—Thank you!

title: I didn’t expect to like this, but it’s great!
text: From my brief use of the Cookshelf app, I can tell that it will be really helpful to me.  I’m not great at Meal Planning, but I think I can manage it better using Cookshelf.  I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea. It’s a great companion to Eat Your Books, which I love,love,love. I think it may take a few days to get Cookshelf figured out, but I look forward to exploring what Cookshelf can do.

title: Essential for cookbook lovers
text: As an avid cook with a cookbook collection of nearly 400, this app is a complete game changer. I can search every recipe in my collection by ingredient!

title: Doesn’t have a big selection
text: I love the idea but most of my cookbooks are not in their data base so you can request to have it indexed or index it yourself . But first you need to read the indexing module. A lot of work but worth it in the end.

title: Traveling Kitchen
text: I’m liking this app so far. Especially when I’m at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep for the night or the week. It is a little disappointing that the ingredients list don’t give to the measurements from the book. But no big deal. It is also handy that it gives you the page number for the recipe so you can go home & pop open the book when ready to start cooking. Now, the only reason for the 4 star rate is because the app had missing ingredients on the list. I made a peanut butter cake from an old Betty Crocker cookbook that my grandma passed down to me, & it was missing items on list. Thankfully it was a recipe I made before discovering this app. Otherwise, a trip back to the store would have been a pain.

title: Love this app
text: I collect cookbooks. Anytime I have a new hobby I must find the cookbook to go with it. This app has changed up how I do dinner as before I had so many cookbooks I started to favor some over the others. Now I search whatever meat I have and try new recipes from books I hadn't opened in awhile or really got to test out.

title: Very disappointed
text: I was so looking forward to upload my favorite books and use the recipes from my phone or tablet. There are no measurements/ingredient amounts showing in the recipes and it admittedly could leave out ingredients. The recipes direct you back to the cookbook. With upgrades, this is a great idea but without the full recipes, it’s not practical at all.

title: Unsure of usage
text: The app asked me for books, then seemed to take a long time to “index” the books I entered. None of them actually ended up getting indexed, and since the app claimed it wouldn’t give recipes, I’m kind of unsure what the app is supposed to do. I enter ingredients and it tells me which cook book to go to? I suppose that could be useful, but so could just flipping to my popular bookmarked pages and buying the ingredients a day ahead.

title: Saved me a another shopping trip
text: I like to plan out my meals for the week and then shop for what I need for those meals. Today at the grocery store, I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available. The whole recipe wasn’t there, but the ingredients I needed to make it were listed. I didn’t have to go home, find another recipe for that night, and make another shopping trip. Yeah!

title: One of a kind great
text: I have so many cookbooks and this is just an amazing tool for getting more use out of them! I love seeing what other people are making from the books I have, and reading their comments. I can search for an ingredient I have and see what recipes I can make. I think the cost is very justified when you consider the work that goes into indexing.

title: Cookbooks Not Indexed
text:
I was so excited to start scanning my cookbooks but the ones I scanned were not indexed yet. Making the app a bit pointless. 
If I am paying for the app I was hoping that my cookbooks would be ready to go.

title: Brilliant but misses it
text: I was knocked out by how easy it was to scan and see my cookbook library appear on the app. Then brokenhearted to realize that it doesn’t have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book, mess with propping it open. I kind of love reading & doing recipes off my tablet, so this is a dealbreaker for me😢

title: My Dreams Came True!
text: As a self proclaimed cookbook fanatic this app is a must! I’m so glad this came to fruition!!!

title: Best app I have
text: I love collecting cookbooks snd recipes, and I finally have a way to search across all of them. It’s like my cookbooks merged with online searching because of this app.  It’s so fun to scroll through everything in one step. I’ve started cooking more since using the app.  Highly recommend.

title: Needs work, steep cost for what it is
text: I saw this advertised on social media and downloaded it immediately—what an awesome idea!! However, I was disappointed to realize you have a 5 book limit for the free version… why would I even pay monthly for the premium? For my own cookbooks? For indexing? The app doesn’t even include the recipes, the UI isn’t great, and the fact that there’s a monthly cost is enraging. I’d rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed to $40/year forever. Ended up deleting because I can’t add all my cookbooks and feels ridiculous to pay for it.

title: no actual recipes in app?
text: I am so confused by this app. I scanned 3 cookbooks, and I don’t see any recipes, only an ingredients list. Makes it quite pointless, no? I’ll be canceling before my free trial is up.

title: Super useful
text: My cookbook collection keeps growing. I used to look at the index of one book after another when I needed a particular recipe.  I might need this app once a month, but it’s still worth the money. Sometimes I just browse random ingredients for fun.

title: Brilliant app
text: CookShelf is fabulous. My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching. That helps immensely with determining whether I can make something-do I have the ingredients or pantry staples or will it require a trip to the market? Worth the subscription.

title: Great idea
text: This is a great idea and I’ve wanted a clearinghouse for my cookbooks. The ingredients lists could be a bit more thorough and I wish that the folks at Mission Control could update indexes more frequently .

title: Great concept, but not there yet
text:
This is a great concept for an app and I want to love it - BUT, I’m guessing because the app is relatively new, only one of my cookbooks is actually indexed (out of the 10 I’ve added to my shelf thus far). I’ve requested indexing for each one, and will update my review with how long it takes for them to be added to the database.  

I’m not super inclined to pay so much for an app that is clearly still so underdeveloped and doesn’t have the capacity to actually do what it says it will do without waiting for them to do updates - so, unless they’re crazy responsive with the indexing, I will probably cancel my trial - though, I may check back at a later date.

title: Love It!
text: This is a game changer! Have a bunch of cannellini beans on hand? No problem. Scroll through every recipe for them in every cookbook you own. Love that the app quickly shows the other ingredients needed, and gives you the page number to reference. Lovely. ❤️

title: Really cool idea
text: Love the concept. But at 40 a year it’s just too much for me. Was a neat app and it was really easy to use!

title: Almost Perfect
text: I have the free version with my top 5 cookbooks loaded. I love being able to quickly find recipes, but even better is quickly searching by ingredient. For example, right now I have an open jar of chilies in adobo sauce to use up. I was able to instantly find quite a few recipes from my collection that use that ingredient. Perfect if you’re at home and trying to plan meals. This isn’t 5 stars for me yet because it doesn’t list quantities. So if I’m looking up a recipe while at the store or away from my cookbooks, I had to guess how much to buy. I’m sure there is licensing reasons for this, but it’s still something to be aware of. Also, I’ll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it’s too expensive to justify.

title: Didn’t know how much I needed this
text: Genius idea!!! I never pay for apps and I got this subscription immediately.

title: Not as advertised
text: It scanned the ISBN’s of my cookbooks but most needed to be indexed into the app and those that were there, tapped on a recipe and it would show me the ingredients but no directions

title: No device sync
text: Doesn’t sync across devices. Pretty disappointing as I use iPad for a lot of cooking

title: Be sure you want this before starting the free trial!
text: This app is a good idea, but it just didn’t work for me.  Extremely disappointed that it auto renewed, and despite my cancelling it as soon as I saw the charge, I still have the privilege of paying $40 for the next year for an app I do not use.    Should I have put it on my calendar? Of course. But life is busy, and I’m disappointed that I can’t even get a prorated refund less than a week after it renewed itself.

title: Great idea, but…
text: Great idea and concept. I downloaded the app and tried scanning several of my cookbooks, you’re limited to 5 until you subscribe.  Anyhow I tried 10 of my cookbooks and only 1 was found. Uninstalled…..maybe down the road I’ll try again……but I doubt it.

title: No more hunting for recipes
text: I wanted a way to catalog all my cookbooks so I could quickly locate recipes, especially ones I’d used previously. So far I’ve scanned over 280 books from my collection (everyone needs a hobby right?) and the app has shown the value already.

title: Boo subscriptions
text: I’d buy it for a one time price - like a cookbook - but never on a subscription.

title: I love this app!!
text: I absolutely love cookbooks. Buts it’s been really hard to use mine because it’s been impossible to search across my collection and know what good and what not. Until now! This app genuinely makes it a joy to collect cookbooks. I can digitally search for recipes across my collections. Read recipe reviews. It’s an absolute must for cookbook enthusiasts. Now I feel like I can expand my collection guilt free! Because I will use them all!

title: Just ok
text: The app does in fact log your books and tracks the recipes. It however does not allow you to take the recipe with you and give you the step by step or quantities. A little misleading. It will give you an ingredient list but that does not always guarantee they are correct. I’ve found a few in the short time I used the free trial that were incorrect. If you added the full recipes to the page it would be worth much more. But this stage is definitely not worth paying for.

title: Game changer for actually using your cookbooks
text: I love being able to search my own cookbooks for things to make rather than googling! It’s nice to be able to just look up an ingredient or dish in the app and know which of my cookbooks have the recipes I need.

title: This price is hard to swallow…
text: It’s obvious from even a small amount of use that this is an amazing resource, but OMG I’m struggling to accept the cost. I’m excited to use it with a first month free deal I got, but imagining keeping it long term makes me grit my teeth because of the cost. Hope they change the way they do things. It’s a wonderful resource, but many home cooks (who often cook partly to save money) will never find out because of the ongoing $$$ drain. Say I love it and want to use it as my personal cooking library index —- in 10 years I’ll have spent $400… and that’s if the price stays the same…

title: Almost perfect
text: Love it but still would like to be able to filter recipes according to ingredients like on Eat your Books!

title: Great in theory, not in Application
text: I’ve been waiting for an app like this for a very long time. And when I got done scanning more than 100 cookbooks, I found out that many of them aren’t indexed or only have one or two recipes indexed. So, a waste of an afternoon and a waste of money.

title: My dream all!!
text: I have over 120 cookbooks so this is something I have been needing for years!!! I will use this everyday and it has already changed the way I cook.  If you love being the kitchen, you need this app.
